function_explanation: Performs a 1D Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) on a specified dimension of a tensor, applying fftshift before and after the transform.
function_docstring:
Performs a 1D Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) on a specified dimension of a tensor, applying fftshift before and after the transform.

Args:
    arr (torch.Tensor): A multi-dimensional tensor on which the 1D FFT is to be performed. The tensor can have any shape, but the FFT will be applied along the specified dimension.
    n (int): The dimension along which to perform the 1D FFT. This should be a valid dimension index of the input tensor `arr`.

Returns:
    torch.Tensor: A tensor of the same shape as `arr`, with the 1D FFT applied along the specified dimension `n`.
usage_example:
import torch
from agent_fft1d import fft1d

# Create a sample tensor
arr = torch.randn(64, 128, 256)

# Perform 1D FFT along the second dimension (index 1)
result = fft1d(arr, 1)

print(result.shape)  # Output will have the same shape as input
